# Tikron — full agent reference

Source-available (Tikron License 1.0), server-authoritative multiplayer SDK for web games — you self-host rooms
on your OWN Cloudflare account (Workers + Durable Objects); no lock-in. One Durable
Object per room, placed at the edge near players. Optional hosted services (leaderboards
+ a usage dashboard, keyed by API key) are the only managed part. You author a Room
subclass (state + intent handlers); clients send intents, the server owns and validates
all state. pnpm + Turborepo monorepo, TypeScript strict, ESM with `.js` import
extensions, `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` on.

Index/docs: llms.txt · AGENTS.md (operational brief)
· docs/PERF.md (measured numbers — never invent latency figures).
Live demos: tikron.dev/agar.html (.io — binary delta + AOI) ·
tikron.dev/shooter.html (FPS — subtick lag-comp + AOI priority tiers, up to 100/room).

## Commands

    pnpm install                                # Node >= 22, pnpm 10.x
    pnpm typecheck | pnpm test | pnpm build     # workspace-wide (turbo)
    pnpm --filter <pkg> dev                     # local edge server (127.0.0.1:8787)
    pnpm --filter <pkg> run deploy              # deploy (once: pnpm exec wrangler login)
    CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN=… CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID=… npm run deploy   # headless/CI deploy, no
                                                # browser login (AGENTS.md "Deploy without a browser")
    npx create-tikron my-game                 # scaffold a standalone game (SDK from npm); ships a
                                                # condensed AGENTS.md + green npm test + vitest.config
                                                # (cloudflare:workers stub); closing msg → tikron.dev/llms-full.txt

## @tikron/server — authoring rooms

### Genre presets (packages/server/src/presets.ts) — pick ONE by genre

    TurnBasedRoom<TState>        turns alternate; JSON sync on mutation; no tick.
                                 Implement: onCreate, onJoin, onLeave.
    CasualRealtimeRoom<TState>   free movement, NO physics tick (cursors/whiteboard/
                                 party). Throttled ~20Hz JSON sync + built-in 30s
                                 reconnection (field: reconnectWindowSec).
                                 Implement: onCreate, onJoin, onSeatExpired(client).
                                 NEVER override onLeave (the preset owns it).
    IoArenaRoom<TState>          fixed-timestep simulation (.io/shooter/racer).
                                 Binary delta sync + acks + optional AOI + reconnection.
                                 queueInputs on (inputs drain at the tick boundary so
                                 onTick sees one consistent batch).
                                 Implement: `protected readonly codec = schema({...})`,
                                 onReady() (setup — the preset owns onCreate),
                                 onTick(dtMs) (state auto-flushes after each tick),
                                 onSeatExpired(client). Fields: tickMs=50, aoi?.
                                 FPS stack (competitive hit reg): lagCompensation=true +
                                 override lagSnapshot() (id->{x,y} to track) + call
                                 rewind(client, input?.ts) in a shoot handler to resolve
                                 the hitscan against the world that client saw; aoi.tiers
                                 for priority updates; client subtickTimestamps for
                                 tick-independent rewind. Demo: apps/gateway ShooterRoom.

### Room<TState> core (packages/server/src/room.ts)

Lifecycle hooks (override): onCreate() · onJoin(client) · onLeave(client) ·
onReconnect(client) · onDispose() · onRestore() (runs after a DO cold-start re-applies
the persisted this.state — rebuild derived in-memory indexes that mirror state but aren't
persisted, e.g. a spatial grid; not called on a fresh/missing/discarded snapshot).

Developer API (protected):
    setState(next)                    replace state + schedule sync
    markStateChanged()                after in-place mutation; coalesced per syncIntervalMs
    onMessage(type, (client, payload, seq?, input?) => ...)   payload is unknown — VALIDATE it;
                                       input.ts = clamped subtick timestamp (server timeline)
                                       when the client opted into subtickTimestamps; pair with
                                       rewind(client, input.ts) for lag-compensated hit checks
    broadcast(type, payload?, exceptClientId?)        dev-defined server→client message
    client.send(type, payload?)       to one client (drops silently while disconnected)
    clientList() / clientCount        seated clients (includes reconnection windows)
    allowReconnection(client, seconds)  inside onLeave; resolves on reattach, rejects on
                                        timeout; requires the client to connect with
                                        ?_session=<key>; survives DO eviction (alarm)
    setSimulationInterval(fn, ms)     fixed tick (realtime only; >= ~20ms)
    clearSimulationInterval()
    enableAOI({viewRadius, mapFields, position, viewer, tiers?})  requires stateCodec;
                                        per-viewer filtered binary sync (anti-wallhack);
                                        grid-indexed (~O(viewers+entities)/flush). tiers =
                                        [{radius,interval}...] ascending: FAR entities
                                        refresh every `interval`th flush (throttled but
                                        never dropped — no flicker); the top downlink lever
                                        at high CCU.
    currentTick                       monotonic sim-tick counter (state-frame tick)
    onError(err, ctx)                 protected, overridable (F120): every caught exception
                                       (throwing handler / onTick / onJoin / onLeave / failed
                                       persist) routes here instead of vanishing; default =
                                       structured console.error `[tikron] room=.. phase=..`.
                                       ctx.phase ∈ onMessage|onTick|onJoin|onLeave|onReconnect|
                                       persist (+ type/clientId when known). A throwing message
                                       handler no longer kills the tick's input batch (F106) —
                                       others still process, ack still sent, onTick still runs.
    forcePersist()                    protected async (F052): immediate durable snapshot (cancels
                                       the coalesce timer) — await after a critical transition to
                                       close the up-to-persistIntervalMs deploy/restart loss window.

tk:stats: a client can send the core-reserved `tk:stats` developer message to read the
room's recent tick/flush timing ({tick, flush, windowMs} of DurationStats); the load test
uses it to report real in-DO processing cost. Not billable, never collides with onMessage.
The reply (PerfSnapshot) also carries drops (DropCounts: rateLimited/staleSeq/oversizedBatch/
unknownType) + errors (cumulative onError count) — the machine-readable "my type is dropped" signal.

Knobs (protected fields): maxClients=Infinity (room-side hard cap → room_full/4002;
reattach never capped) · maxInputsPerSecond=30 (excess inputs dropped) ·
syncIntervalMs=50 (0 = immediate microtask flush — unbounded, avoid) ·
sendAcks=false (ack input seq for client reconciliation) · queueInputs=false (drain inputs
at the tick boundary; needs a sim interval; IoArenaRoom sets it) · stateCodec (binary delta) ·
persistIntervalMs=5000 (durable snapshot cadence) · stateVersion=1 (bump on incompatible
TState change + override migrateState(from, old) so a redeploy migrates old snapshots
instead of silently restoring the old shape; return null = discard + start fresh).

Facts: Client = { id, data (server-only scratch), auth?, send }. client.id is the session key
when supplied, else the connection id — state keyed by client.id survives reconnects.
client.auth = {id, claims} is the DURABLE verified identity (present when onAuth returns an
object) — key leaderboards/bans/ownership on client.auth?.id ?? client.id, NEVER the raw
session id (a new tab/reconnect resets it); live-only (not persisted across eviction).
Only this.state and client.data survive DO eviction (persisted snapshot); ad-hoc
instance fields do not. State must be structured-clone-able. Duplicate session connect
= takeover (old socket closed 4001). Input seq replay-floor resets on reattach.

### defineRoom (packages/server/src/define-room.ts)

    export const MyRoom = defineRoom(MyRoomImpl, options?)
    // wrangler.jsonc: durable_objects.bindings [{ name: "MyRoom", class_name: "MyRoom" }]
    // URL party = kebab-case of the binding: MyRoom -> /parties/my-room/<room-id>

DefineRoomOptions (all optional):
    reportOccupancy(env, {roomId, count, sessions, seq, projectId?, messages?})
    validateSession(env, {roomId, session}) -> boolean     gate self-supplied ?_session=
    onAuth(env, {roomId, projectId?, token, session}) -> AuthResult   player JWT gate (?_auth=).
        AuthResult = boolean | {id?, claims?}: an object populates client.auth={id, claims}
        (id = res.id ?? claims.sub); true = authorize anon (client.auth undefined); false = 4004.
    services.submitScore(env, {...}, entry)   self-host leaderboard hook — wire
        platformLeaderboard({apiKey:(env)=>env.TIKRON_API_KEY}); room calls
        this.services.leaderboard?.submit({board, playerId, score, displayName?, mode?})
        (mode max|sum|last). tk_live_ secret key server-side; no key = silent no-op; POSTs
        fire-and-forget to https://tikron.dev/api/ingest/score (never throws into the room).
RoomContext.devMode = env.DEV_MODE === "1" (verbose per-drop warns gated on it; the unknown-
message-type warn fires once/type regardless, so a typo'd onMessage type shows in wrangler tail).

Close codes (exported consts): 4001 CLOSE_SESSION_TAKEN_OVER · 4002 CLOSE_ROOM_FULL ·
4003 CLOSE_INVALID_SESSION · 4004 CLOSE_UNAUTHORIZED.

## @tikron/client — browser SDK

    const client = new GameClient(location.host, {
      party: "my-room",            // kebab-case binding name
      apiKey?: "tk_pub_...",       // PUBLISHABLE key for browser connect/matchmake/read (?apiKey=).
                                   // Two prefix-derived classes: tk_pub_ (browser-safe) vs tk_live_
                                   // (secret, server-side ingest only — never ship in client code).
      authToken?: "<jwt>",         // player token (?_auth=) when project requires it
      stateCodec?: MySchema,       // must match the room's codec for binary sync
      subtickTimestamps?: true,    // stamp each send() with its server-clock time so the
                                   // room can rewind lag comp to the input instant (needs
                                   // clock sync, on by default); FPS hit registration
      inputBatchMs?: 33,           // coalesce a burst of sends into one c:mbatch frame
                                   // (~1 tick); 0 = send each immediately. Needs a 0.2+
                                   // server (older servers drop multi-input batches)
      networkConditions?: {...},   // DEV ONLY: simulate latency/jitter/loss to feel the
                                   // game under a bad network (latencyMs, jitterMs,
                                   // lossRate, seed); loss drops only binary state frames
      reconnect?: {...},           // backoff policy (default 500ms base, doubling, 5 + jitter)
    });
    const m = await client.matchmake({ mode?, maxClients?, region? }); // {roomId, sessionId, region?}
    const room = await client.joinOrCreate(m.roomId, { _session: m.sessionId });
    // Since 0.6 joinOrCreate validates the Welcome frame and rejects codec/protocol drift with
    // RoomJoinError("protocol_mismatch"|"schema_mismatch") naming BOTH sides' values (via
    // schemaFingerprint over the codec shape; str(maxLen) changes don't trip it). Live-reconnect
    // mismatch → console.error + close (no retry). Fingerprint check only active vs a 0.6+ server.

    room.send(type, payload)             auto-seq'd intent
    room.onStateChange(state => ...)     authoritative state (JSON or decoded binary)
    room.onMessage(type, payload => ...) dev-defined server messages
    room.onAck(seq => ...) / room.lastAckSeq   for reconciliation (sendAcks rooms)
    room.connectionId                    stable id (== session key when supplied)
    room.leave()

Netcode helpers: InputPredictor (predict/reconcile — input replay for server-integrated
movement) · SnapshotBuffer (interpolation, delayMs ~100). Transport auto-reconnects
(PartySocket); it stops on close 4001. Binary frames arrive as arraybuffer (SDK sets
binaryType — hand-rolled clients must too). Decode guards: a binary state frame that fails
to decode → console.error ONCE, frame dropped, last good state kept; a binary frame with no
stateCodec set → console.warn ONCE (this covers a pre-0.6 server that sends no fingerprint).

Render helpers (client-authoritative movement — "client sends position, server validates
speed"; anti-rubber-band by contract):

    // shared by room AND client bundle — one budget, two sides (@tikron/sim type)
    const PROFILE: MotionProfile = { maxSpeed: 500, tolerance: 1.15, stepMs: 50,
                                     world: 3000, sendHeadroom: 1.1 };
    const motion = RenderPredictor.fromProfile(spawnPlaceholder, PROFILE);
    const smoother = new EntitySmoother();
    room.onMessage("rejected", (p) => motion.correct(p as Vec2));  // rebases the clamp
    room.onStateChange((s) => { const me = s.players[room.connectionId];
      if (me) { motion.alive = me.alive; motion.reconcile(me); } });
    setInterval(() => room.send("move", motion.sendPosition(room.clock.serverNow())),
      PROFILE.stepMs);                       // the ONLY value that may go on the wire
    // per render frame: camera/self = motion.frame(dirX, dirY, dtMs)  (1:1, no easing);
    // per remote entity: smoother.update(id, {x,y,angle}, dtMs); then smoother.prune(seen)
    // SERVER move handler: resolveMovement(prev, target, PROFILE, deltaMs) — partial
    // advance on reject (never freeze); send "rejected" {x,y} back.

RenderPredictor: continuous frame-rate integration + decaying correction offset + a
budget-clamped sendPosition(serverNow) choke point (measured elapsed time, capped 2
ticks; sendHeadroom in (1, tolerance) so clamped sends always fit the server budget and
backlogs drain). reconcile(echo) ignores small RTT gaps; snaps on first frame / respawn
(set .alive) / >= snapDistance. EntitySmoother: exponential position+shortest-arc angle
easing over SnapshotBuffer samples (rounds off AOI priority-tier steps); prune(seen)
per frame. Pure primitives also exported: integrateMove, clampToBudget, decayOffset,
applyCorrection, smoothAxis, smoothAngle, followCamera.

Casual (server-authoritative) rooms use EntitySmoother ALONE — no RenderPredictor/SnapshotBuffer:
smoother.update(id,{x,y},dtMs) per remote entity + smoother.prune(seen); render the local player
straight from its own input target (zero added latency). Default snapDistance=300 never triggers
in a normalized [0,1] world, so it only eases — exactly what a cursor/paint room wants.
Input: the starter client uses pointer events (pointermove/pointerdown) + canvas touch-action:none
+ viewport maximum-scale=1 — one code path for touch & mouse (the absolute-cursor pattern).

## @tikron/sim — shared movement math (zero-dep, isomorphic)

    stepToward(pos, target, maxSpeed, dtMs)            budget-capped step (client+server)
    validateMovement(prev, req, cfg, deltaMs)          accept or freeze-reject (legacy)
    resolveMovement(prev, req, cfg, deltaMs)           accept or PARTIAL-ADVANCE + rejected:
                                                       true — use this in move handlers (a
                                                       frozen position turns one reject into
                                                       an RTT-long rubber-band cascade)
    integrateMove(pos, dirX, dirY, dtMs, maxSpeed,     per-frame continuous integration
                  world, maxDtMs)                      (normalized diagonals, dt+world clamp)
    clampToBudget(lastSent, next, profile, elapsedMs)  outgoing-send speed-budget clamp
    MotionProfile = MovementConfig + { stepMs, world?, sendHeadroom? }  — define ONCE in a
    module both sides import; keep world == the codec's quant range.

## @tikron/schema — binary delta state sync

    import { schema, mapOf, quant, type Codec } from "@tikron/schema";
    const MySchema: Codec<MyState> = schema({
      players: mapOf(schema({
        x: quant(0, 2000, 0.1),  // fixed-grid quantize: 1-4 bytes vs f32's 4, and
        y: quant(0, 2000, 0.1),  // sub-step jitter drops out of deltas. THE bandwidth
        score: "u32",            // lever for continuous fields (pos/vel/hp/angle).
      })),                       // string-keyed maps: per-entry delta
      round: "u16",              // prims: u8 u16 u32 i32 f32 f64 bool str
    });
    // server: protected readonly codec = MySchema   (IoArenaRoom)
    // client: new GameClient(host, { stateCodec: MySchema })

Builders (each -> Codec<T>, T inferred): schema(shape) · prim(type) ·
quant(min,max,step) (u8/u16/u32 by level count; round-trip error <= step/2) ·
mapOf(child) · listOf(child) · optionalOf(child) (nullable) · enumOf(...values) (u8
index, <=256) · str(maxLen) (length-checked string). Wire ops: encodeFull, encodeDelta,
encodeDeltaOrNull (delta or null when unchanged — the AOI hot path), decodeFull, applyDelta.

Wire: first byte 0x01 full snapshot / 0x02 delta; decodeFull/applyDelta client-side
(the SDK does this). Schema must be identical (and same order) on both sides — a fingerprint
handshake now ENFORCES this: drift surfaces as RoomJoinError("schema_mismatch") at joinOrCreate
(naming both fingerprints) instead of silently decoding garbage. schemaFingerprint(codec) ->
u32|null (FNV-1a over the codec shape; null = undescribable → check skipped). str(maxLen) is
write-only (not decode-relevant), so a str length change does NOT trip it.

## Platform (apps/gateway) — multi-tenancy

REST: GET /api/matchmake?type=<party>&mode=&max= → {roomId, sessionId} (403
cap_room_hours | cap_concurrent_rooms when capped) · GET /api/rooms → lobby ·
Dashboard API under /api/platform/* (session-cookie auth; see dashboard).

Keys: two prefix-derived classes — tk_pub_ (publishable: browser connect/matchmake/leaderboard
read; safe to ship) and tk_live_ (secret: + server-side ingest; keep off the client). SHA-256
stored over the WHOLE key incl. prefix (scope is unforgeable); shown once. Create: POST
/api/platform/projects/:id/keys {scope?:"public"|"secret"} (default public; response includes
scope). /parties/* connects require ?apiKey= when the platform DB is bound; missing key falls back
to the metered demo project (DEMO_PROJECT_ID) if configured; invalid key → 401. Local dev:
DEV_MODE=1 bypasses. Caps (D1 config, defaults): 1,000 room-hours/month · 20 concurrent rooms · 20
players/room. Usage: rooms report occupancy + 30s heartbeats; matchmaker accrues
room-hours/peak CCU/messages → usage_daily (60s alarm flush).
Self-host leaderboard ingest: POST /api/ingest/score (Authorization: Bearer tk_live_ ONLY;
tk_pub_ → 403 key_scope_forbidden) {board, playerId, score, displayName?, mode?} → 204 |
{error, message}; caps 403 cap_leaderboard_boards (free_leaderboard_boards=50, new boards only) /
429 cap_leaderboard_rate (isolate-local bucket 30/s, burst 60). Read: GET /api/leaderboard?board=
&limit=&apiKey=tk_pub_ (Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *).

## Hard rules (failures these prevent — details in AGENTS.md)

1. Server owns state; clients send intents. Validate every payload (type-guard +
   clamp); act on the server's view, never the client's claim.
2. Party name = kebab-case of the DO binding, everywhere.
3. Local wrangler only (pnpm scripts); old global wrangler breaks partyserver.
4. Never benchmark multi-room on local workerd (single process): 8×16 rooms local
   p50 645ms vs deployed 82ms (PERF.md).
5. Don't lower syncIntervalMs casually: event RATE, not bytes, kills rooms — the
   50ms coalesce took a 64-player room from 58/64 dropped sockets to 0 (PERF.md).
6. Turn-based guardrail: core/TurnBasedRoom rooms must never need a tick.

## Measured (docs/PERF.md, deployed on real Cloudflare, client in Korea)

20-player AOI room: ack p50 ~85ms (≈80ms network + single-digit engine), p99 ~115ms,
3.8 KiB/s/client. 128 CCU across 8 rooms: p50 ~82ms — rooms scale horizontally.
100 players in ONE room (deployed, 10s join ramp): 100/100 connected, 0 closes, ack p50
97ms/p99 144ms, server tick+flush 0ms (tk:stats) — the full FPS stack (subtick shots +
tiered AOI + map deltas) holds too. Caveat: 100 upgrades in 3s to one DO fail ~20% —
pace joins (~10s) / rely on client reconnect backoff. Turn-based idle: ~77 B/s/client.
Room cost: DO duration ≈ $0.005625/room-hour (128MB pinned, verified) PLUS inbound WebSocket
request billing (20:1 rule applies to the standard non-hibernation WS too), which DOMINATES at
realtime CCU — a 20Hz room ≈ $0.016 / $0.040 / $0.060 per room-hour at 20 / 64 / 100 CCU (marginal,
past the free tier). Self-hosted rooms bill to YOUR Cloudflare account. Hibernation saves $0 with
an always-on tick. Full math + verified-vs-estimated: docs/COSTS.md.

## Limits & roadmap (v1 — full detail in AGENTS.md "Limits & roadmap")

- Transport: WebSocket only (TCP) — head-of-line blocking under packet loss; fine for
  turn-based / casual / moderate .io, NOT competitive twitch shooters. WebTransport is
  roadmap (CF Workers don't terminate it server-side yet — no date).
- Deploys restart DOs; rooms recover via the durable snapshot + 30s session reconnection
  (players reland in the same seat/state). State-SHAPE migration across versions: bump
  stateVersion + override migrateState(from, old) to transform an old snapshot on restore
  (return null = discard + start fresh, with a warning) — no longer a silent old-shape restore.
- DO placement is fixed near the room's creator; cross-region groups get asymmetric latency.
  Place a room's DO near a geography with a matchmake region hint (region= one of wnam/enam/
  weur/eeur/apac/oc/afr/me, applied when the room is created); still matchmake by region so
  members are near each other and the placement.
- Matchmaking = joinOrCreate + reservation + filterBy + lobby list ONLY. No skill/MMR,
  parties, or reconnect-into-queue — build ranked/party logic in your app layer.
- Scale: measured comfortable at 20 players/room, 100 in one room, and 128 CCU across rooms
  (PERF.md); rooms scale horizontally. AOI is now grid-indexed (~O(viewers+entities)/flush,
  property-tested vs the naive filter) + priority tiers — not O(n²) — so the per-room ceiling
  at high CCU is connection admission + fan-out, not filtering. Shard very large worlds anyway.
- Wire compat: client and server share PROTOCOL_VERSION + the binary frame layout — ship the
  SAME SDK minor on both sides; 0.1.x and 0.2.x are not wire-compatible (0.2 added c:mbatch,
  subtick ts, and the state-frame serverTime). Upgrade both together. Since 0.6 a schema-
  fingerprint handshake enforces codec parity (RoomJoinError("schema_mismatch"|"protocol_mismatch")
  on initial-join drift, naming both sides); protection needs a 0.6+ server, and a client-first
  rollout is safe (a new client just skips the check against an old server).
